product.name: Aramid Vis 250
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Aramid Viscose Fr Woven Workwear Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: Aramid Viscose Fr Fabric, 53% Viscose Fr 40% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 250 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 250 g/m², 53% Viscose Fr 40% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric Aramid Viscose Fr twill fabric
descriptions: 250 g/m², 53% Viscose Fr, 40% m-Aramid, 5% p-Aramid, 2% Carbon.
for oil and gas industry workers.
With viscose fr content is suitable to use in outdoor very hot climates. Inherent flame retardant fabric with EN ISO 11612 certification.
Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
Resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
Aramid Viscose Fr Fabric, 53% Viscose Fr 40% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 250 gr/m'2
250 g/m², 53% Viscose Fr 40% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric Aramid Viscose Fr twill fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 53% Viscose fr (cv fr) 40% m-Aramid (m-AR) 5% p-Aramid (p-AR) 2% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp > 1200N; Weft > 1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>60N ; Weft>50N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Product description explicitly states intended use for oil and gas industry workers, directly tying it to the energy sector.
Technical parameter explicitly lists 'Firefighters Costumes' as an application, and the product image label references a flame retardant suit, directly supporting firefighting use.
No explicit military or uniformed-service reference despite protective FR properties.
FR and chemical resistance could suit industrial metalworking, but no explicit welding or foundry use is stated.

evidence: for oil and gas industry workers.

categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Aramid Viscose Fr Woven Workwear Fabrics
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly described as intended for oil and gas industry workers, with general industrial protective properties (FR, antistatic, arc, chemical resistance) typical of workwear rather than a specific named sector like firefighting alone. The category source group also explicitly labels it as workwear fabric.

evidence: applicationImageLabels: Viscose fr flame retardant suit
secondary.rationale: A technical parameter explicitly lists 'Firefighters Costumes' as an application, and the FR suit imagery supports a genuine secondary use in firefighter protective clothing, independent of the dominant oil-and-gas workwear framing.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Oil and gas industry reference is present but the product is framed as general protective workwear rather than a utility/electrical energy sector application; antistatic/arc properties alone are insufficient per rules.
No explicit welding, foundry or metal-splash use stated despite FR and heat protection properties.
No explicit military or uniformed-service reference in the product data.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Product data contains competing explicit signals (oil and gas workwear vs. firefighter costume application) without a single dominant framing, requiring human confirmation of primary/secondary split.
